formulas.formula:
Here's a comprehensive formula sheet for CBSE Class 11 Chemistry - The s-Block Elements:

```json
[
  {
    "name": "Position in the periodic table",
    "formula": "s-Block elements are located in Group 1 (alkali metals) and Group 2 (alkaline earth metals) of the periodic table",
    "units": "",
    "notes": "Identify the position of s-Block elements in the periodic table"
  },
  {
    "name": "Electronic configuration of s-Block elements",
    "formula": "ns1, ns2, ns2np1 (n = 1, 2, 3, ...)",
    "variables": "n = principal quantum number",
    "units": "",
    "notes": "Write the electronic configuration of s-Block elements using the formula"
  },
  {
    "name": "Valency of s-Block elements",
    "formula": "Valency = number of electrons in the outermost shell",
    "variables": "",
    "units": "",
    "notes": "Determine the valency of s-Block elements using the formula"
  },
  {
    "name": "Anomalous valency of Be",
    "formula": "Be has a valency of +2 due to half-filled or completely filled orbital",
    "variables": "",
    "units": "",
    "notes": "Explain the anomalous valency of Be"
  },
  {
    "name": "Physical properties of s-Block elements",
    "formula": "They are silvery-white metals with low melting and boiling points",
    "variables": "",
    "units": "",
    "notes": "Describe the physical properties of s-Block elements"
  },
  {
    "name": "Chemical properties of s-Block elements",
    "formula": "They are highly reactive metals with a tendency to lose one or two electrons to form a +1 or +2 ion",
    "variables": "",
    "units": "",
    "notes": "Explain the chemical properties of s-Block elements"
  },
  {
    "name": "Alkali metals show +1 oxidation state",
    "formula": "They have a tendency to lose one electron to form a +1 ion",
    "variables": "",
    "units": "",
    "notes": "Discuss the oxidation state of alkali metals"
  },
  {
    "name": "Alkaline earth metals show +2 oxidation state",
    "formula": "They have a tendency to lose two electrons to form a +2 ion",
    "variables": "",
    "units": "",
    "notes": "Discuss the oxidation state of alkaline earth metals"
  },
  {
    "name": "Reactivity of s-Block elements",
    "formula": "Their reactivity increases down the group",
    "variables": "",
    "units": "",
    "notes": "Explain the trend of reactivity in s-Block elements"
  },
  {
    "name": "Preparation of alkali metals",
    "formula": "They can be prepared by electrolysis of their fused halides or by the reaction of their oxides with carbon",
    "variables": "",
    "units": "",
    "notes": "Describe the methods of preparation of alkali metals"
  },
  {
    "name": "Preparation of alkaline earth metals",
    "formula": "They can be prepared by electrolysis of their fused halides or by the reaction of their oxides with carbon",
    "variables": "",
    "units": "",
    "notes": "Describe the methods of preparation of alkaline earth metals"
  },
  {
    "name": "Chemical reactivity of s-Block elements",
    "formula": "They react with water to produce hydrogen gas and their respective hydroxides",
    "variables": "",
    "units": "",
    "notes": "Describe the chemical reactivity of s-Block elements with water"
  },
  {
    "name": "Reaction of alkali metals with water",
    "formula": "2M + 2H2O → 2MOH + H2",
    "variables": "M = alkali metal",
    "units": "",
    "notes": "Write the chemical equation for the reaction of alkali metals with water"
  },
  {
    "name": "Reaction of alkaline earth metals with water",
    "formula": "M + 2H2O → M(OH)2 + H2",
    "variables": "M = alkaline earth metal",
    "units": "",
    "notes": "Write the chemical equation for the reaction of alkaline earth metals with water"

    "name": "Reaction of s-Block elements with acids",
